_comment: Kiro host-specific wrapper config (Kiro 0.9+). Skills are generated ONCE to the repo-root skills/ (shared by all hosts) and the CLI copies them to .kiro/skills/ on install; this adapter only describes Kiro's wrappers: custom subagents (with Kiro's read/write/shell/web tool vocab) for the roles roles.json marks subagent:true, a lean auto-steering orchestrator, manual hooks, and a disabled example MCP config. Authored (not generated): README.md.

steering.name: devloop
steering.description: DevLoop business-analysis chain — turning a feature idea into requirements, INVEST user stories, a story review, and a Jira organization plan, grounded in compiled LLM-Wikis. Use when the user asks to gather requirements, write stories/tickets, or plan a backlog.

hooks.name: DevLoop: lint wikis
hooks.description: Validate every DevLoop wiki for broken [[wikilinks]] and orphans, including cross-wiki [[namespace:Concept]] links. Run manually from the Agent Hooks panel.

hooks.name: DevLoop: sync wikis
hooks.description: Clone/pull every git-backed wiki and report which sources changed since the last build (exits non-zero on a git failure). Run manually from the Agent Hooks panel.

mcp._note: Example only — installed to .kiro/settings/mcp.json ONLY IF that file is absent (never clobbers your real MCP config). Point `args` at the Microsoft Graph / SharePoint MCP server you use, set `disabled` to false, and provide the token via the referenced env var (never hardcode secrets).
